title: JK Flip-Flop (Functional Block Diagram)
examTip: JK FF is universal: acts as SR FF (using J,K like S,R), D FF (tie J=D, K=D-bar), T FF (tie J=K=1). PRE and CLR are asynchronous active-LOW inputs. IC: 74LS76 is a dual JK FF.

blocks.label: JK Truth Table
sublabels: J=0,K=0 → No Change | J=0,K=1 → Q=0 (Reset) | J=1,K=0 → Q=1 (Set) | J=1,K=1 → Toggle

theory:
The JK Flip-Flop block symbol is the standard schematic representation used in timing diagrams, sequential circuit schematics, and system-level designs. It abstracts the complex NAND gate implementation into a clean, readable block with labeled pins.

Pin Functions: J (Jump/Set) and K (Kill/Reset) are the two data inputs that control the next state. CLK is the clock input with a dynamic indicator ('>') — in negative edge-triggered variants (like 74LS76), a bubble appears at the CLK pin. PRE-bar (Preset) is an asynchronous active-LOW input that immediately sets Q=1 regardless of J, K, or CLK. CLR-bar (Clear) is an asynchronous active-LOW input that immediately resets Q=0.

Truth Table (Synchronous Operation, when PRE=CLR=1): J=0, K=0 → No Change (memory function); J=0, K=1 → Q=0 (Reset); J=1, K=0 → Q=1 (Set); J=1, K=1 → Toggle (Q complements). This truth table eliminates the forbidden state present in SR flip-flops.

Universality as a Building Block: The JK FF is called 'universal' because all other flip-flop types can be derived from it. To make it behave as a D FF: connect K=D-bar (J=D). To make it a T FF: connect J=K=T. To make it an SR FF: use J as S and K as R (while avoiding J=K=1).

IC 74LS76: Contains two independent JK flip-flops with individual asynchronous PRE-bar and CLR-bar inputs. It is negative edge-triggered, meaning state changes occur on the falling edge of the clock signal.
keyPoints: Five left-side inputs: J, CLK, K (synchronous), PRĒ, CLR̄ (asynchronous)
Two right-side outputs: Q (true) and Q̄ (complement) — always available simultaneously
Toggle mode (J=K=1) is the unique and defining feature of the JK flip-flop
PRĒ=0 forces Q=1 asynchronously; CLR̄=0 forces Q=0 asynchronously — active LOW
74LS76: dual JK FF IC, negative edge-triggered
Universal flip-flop: can be configured to behave as D, T, or SR flip-flop
useCases: Synchronous and ripple counter design using toggle mode
Control logic in digital state machines requiring Set, Reset, Hold, and Toggle operations
Configurable register elements that need multiple operating modes in a single IC
